scenario: Pinnacle Insurance triages 8000 claims tasks per week through Camunda 8.8 Tasklist. Adjusters open the Tasks page to scan their queue, and the team lead wants the tile preview for each task to surface enough information that adjusters can pick the next item without opening any tasks first.
question: Which fields does the Tasklist task-tile preview show for each task in the queue?

options.text: Task name, name of the process the task belongs to, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow-up date
Task name, name of the process, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, the task variables payload, and the linked BPMN element id
Task name, name of the process, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and the operator who last reassigned the task in Tasklist
Task name, name of the process, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and the SLA timer remaining until breach expressed in hours

a.text: Correct. The Tasks queue tile preview surfaces task name, process name, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow-up date per the docs.

b.text: Incorrect. The tile preview does not include the task variables payload or the linked BPMN element id; those internal references are not part of the queue summary view.

c.text: Incorrect. The tile preview shows the current assignee, not a last-reassigner audit field; the documented attributes do not include any reassignment history on the tile.

d.text: Incorrect. The tile preview lists raw due-date and follow-up-date timestamps rather than a derived SLA countdown; no breach-timer field is documented for the queue tile.

explanation: The Tasks queue in Tasklist shows a preview of each available task with task name, process name, task context description, assignee, priority, creation date, due date, and follow-up date.
